item.title: Ba beat Labasa 1-0 to book place in Fiji FACT final
item.description: Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026 Ba has booked its place in the BiC Fiji FACT final after defeating Labasa 1-0 in a thrilling semi-final at Subrail Park.
item.content:encoded:
Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026

Ba has booked its place in the BiC Fiji FACT final after defeating Labasa 1-0 in a thrilling semi-final at Subrail Park.

Both teams produced an entertaining display of football, with Ba dominating possession in the opening half and creating several scoring opportunities through their impressive passing game.

However, they were unable to capitalise on their chances.

Labasa also looked dangerous on the counterattack and created opportunities of their own, but neither side managed to break the deadlock as the match remained scoreless at half-time.

The second half continued at a fast pace, with both teams pushing hard for the opening goal.

Despite several attacking moves from both sides, neither team was able to find the back of the net, forcing the match into extra time after a goalless 90 minutes.

Ba finally made the breakthrough in the first spell of extra time when Etonia Dogalau scored what proved to be the winning goal to put the Men in Black ahead 1-0.

Labasa's hopes of staging a comeback suffered a major setback in the second spell of extra time after Iliesa Nayasi was shown a red card, reducing the Babasiga Lions to 10 men.

Ba defended resolutely for the remainder of the match to seal the victory and secure a place in tomorrow's BIC Fiji FACT final.

Following the win, Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says he was extremely proud of the team's performance.

Dayal praised the players for their outstanding display and expressed confidence that the BIC Fiji FACT title would return to Ba.

He also calls on Ba supporters to turn out in large numbers for the final and thanked the loyal fans who packed Subrail Park , creating a vibrant atmosphere with their musical instruments and unwavering support throughout the match.

Ba will now await the winner of the second semi-final as they look to lift the BIC Fiji FACT trophy.

Meanwhile, the final will be played tomorrow at 3pm.

item.title: Spain edge Uruguay 1-0 to finish Group H in FIFA World Cup
item.description: Spain secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Uruguay in their final FIFA World Cup Group H match. Spain led 1-0 at half-time and held on to claim all three points, while Uruguay finished the match with 10 men after a late red card in stoppage time.
item.content:encoded:
Spain secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Uruguay in their final FIFA World Cup Group H match.

Spain led 1-0 at half-time and held on to claim all three points, while Uruguay finished the match with 10 men after a late red card in stoppage time.

In other matches, Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia finished 0-0, France beat Norway 4-1, and Senegal thrashed Iraq 5-0.

Egypt face Iran and New Zealand take on Belgium at 3.00pm.

item.title: Navua FC determined to end long BiC Fiji FACT Title drought
item.description: Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026 Navua FC is aiming to create history by bringing the prestigious BiC Fiji FACT trophy back home after many years.
item.content:encoded:
Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026

Navua FC is aiming to create history by bringing the prestigious BiC Fiji FACT trophy back home after many years.

Speaking ahead of today's second semi-final, Navua head coach Roderic Singh says the club has not won the Fiji FACT title for many years, and the players are determined to end that drought.

Singh believes the semi-final against Lautoka will be a tough contest but says his side are ready for the challenge.

He says the players worked extremely hard during the pool matches to secure their place in the semi-finals and are grateful for the opportunity to compete for a place in the final.

The Navua coach adds that the squad are excited and highly motivated ahead of the crucial clash .

Insert: Singh on missed opporunities 27th June.

Singh is also calling on Navua supporters to keep the team in their prayers and continue backing them throughout the tournament.

Meanwhile, the first semi-final kicks off at 2.30pm, with Labasa taking on Ba and Navua taking Lautoka at 4.30pm.

item.title: One-test All Black joins Welsh club
item.description: Another All Black is heading offshore.After seven seasons, Crusaders loose froward Cullen Grace will join Welsh club Scarlets afte

item.title: Drua Women ready to go against Reds
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are ready to go against the Reds today in Ballymore as a win today will secure them a home semi final. During their Captain's run yesterday, former Drua Women's Head Coach Mosese Rauluni along with former players Ana Korovata and Siteri Rasolea came out with others to show their support and cheer the girls on.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are ready to go against the Reds today in Ballymore as a win today will secure them a home semi final.

During their Captain's run yesterday, former Drua Women's Head Coach Mosese Rauluni along with former players Ana Korovata and Siteri Rasolea came out with others to show their support and cheer the girls on.

The Drua Women lead the Super Rugby Women’s points table with 9 points, followed closely by the Waratahs on 8 points, while the Western Force sit third with 5 points.

The Brumbies are fourth with 4 points, while the Reds are at the bottom of the standings with 2 points.

Thehe Drua Women will take on the Reds at 4.05pm today.

In other games, the Force will face the Waratahs at 6.05pm.

item.title: Lelean continues Eastern Zone dominance in U17 grade
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 26/06/2026 Lelean Memorial School continued its dominance in the Vodafone Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U17 competition with a 21-10 victory over Tailevu North College at St Joseph the Worker Primary School grounds.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 26/06/2026

Lelean Memorial School continued its dominance in the Vodafone Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U17 competition with a 21-10 victory over Tailevu North College at St Joseph the Worker Primary School grounds.

Lelean displayed strong teamwork and determination throughout the match to overcome a spirited Tailevu North College outfit and claim an important win in the competition.

The victory was made even more special as the team celebrated the 17th birthday of teammate Orisi Moceituba from Nasoqo Village in Naitasiri.

Following the match, players and supporters joined in celebrating Moceituba's birthday, making it a memorable day as he marked both a personal milestone and a team victory.

Moceituba's mother, Lolohea Koroi, was also on the sidelines and joined in the celebrations with the team.

In another U17 result, Nakasi High School defeated Muaniweni Secondary School 27-3.

Meanwhile , Lelean U17 will take on Nakasi High School at Bau College Ground at 2.15pm tomorrow, while RKS will face Sila Central High School at 12.25pm at Burebasaga Ground.

In the U18 grade, Sila Central will face St John's College at 12.15pm tomorrow at Kings Ground, while RKS will take on Lelean at 3.05pm.

The Eastern Zone competition continues tomorrow at Kings Ground, Burebasaga Ground and Bau College Ground.

item.title: Australia held to 0-0 draw by Paraguay in Group D clash
item.description: Australia and Paraguay settled for a nil-all draw in their FIFA World Cup Group D clash after neither side could find a breakthrough. The match remained goalless at half-time, with both teams unable to convert their chances, and the deadlock continued throughout the second half as the points were shared.
item.content:encoded:
Australia and Paraguay settled for a nil-all draw in their FIFA World Cup Group D clash after neither side could find a breakthrough.

The match remained goalless at half-time, with both teams unable to convert their chances, and the deadlock continued throughout the second half as the points were shared.

Meanwhile, in other matches, Curaçao lost 2-0 to Ivory Coast, Ecuador beat Germany 2-1, Tunisia lost 3-1 to the Netherlands, Japan drew 1-1 with Sweden, and Türkiye beat the USA 3-2.

In tomorrow's matches, Norway will face France and Senegal will take on Iraq at 7.00am, while Cabo Verde will play Saudi Arabia and Uruguay will meet Spain at 12.00pm, before Egypt face IR Iran and New Zealand take on Belgium at 3.00pm.

item.title: Home semi-final spot driving Drua Women ahead of crucial Reds clash
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's Head Coach Mike Legge says they have made only one positional change to the side ahead of their cruci

item.title: Consistency key as Drua Women chase semi-final spot
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's Head Coach Mike Legge says while the team's ultimate aspiration is to win the Super Rugby Women's com
